title: ActiveMQ Broker Administrative Shutdown Audit
dataSources: ActiveMQ broker log shutdown banners and JMX stop hooks (`sourcetype=activemq:log`)
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder file monitor; optional JMX notifications to the same index with `sourcetype=activemq:broker`
spl:
index=messaging sourcetype="activemq:log"
| search "Stopping broker" OR "Stopped broker" OR "Shutting down" OR "JVM received" OR ("BrokerService" AND "stop")
| table _time, host, _raw
description: Broker stop sequences document who interrupted message flow—critical when proving whether downtime was malicious, mistaken, or maintenance.
value: Supplements change tickets with ground-truth timestamps for SOX/ITGC style controls on messaging infrastructure.
implementation: Ensure graceful shutdown emits identifiable log lines in your distribution. Correlate with privileged OS accounts and bastion session logs. Retain per records-management policy.
visualization: Timeline (shutdown events), Table (host, operator context if present), Single value (events per month).
grandmaExplanation: We keep proof of when the message server was deliberately turned off, which matters for investigations and formal audits.
